title: Is LCL nutritous
wikitext:
[]: Is LCL nutritous?             Quote Post by Gendos Glasses » Mon Jul 22, 2024 8:43 pm Hello there, I was asking myself if being in lcl is nutritous. Could I stop eating if I kept diving in fresh lcl?

[]: Re: Is LCL nutritous?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed Jul 24, 2024 10:35 am It's probably as nutritious as amniotic fluid XD

[]: Re: Is LCL nutritous?             Quote Post by Horizon » Sun Feb 09, 2025 8:33 pm There is a part of me that feels like it tastes like orange juice but there is another part of me that thinks that its probably engineered for efficiency over taste, so it probably tastes either like nothing or like chemicals. Anyway eva branded orange juice when?

[]: Re: Is LCL nutritous?             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon Feb 10, 2025 5:08 pm Horizon wrote: There is a part of me that feels like it tastes like orange juice but there is another part of me that thinks that its probably engineered for efficiency over taste, so it probably tastes either like nothing or like chemicals. Anyway eva branded orange juice when?

[]: Re: Is LCL nutritous?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Feb 16, 2025 4:45 pm It would make a kind of sense, since it's supposedly "the source of life" and has the same composition as a person's body

[]: Re: Is LCL nutritous?             Quote Post by nearsecondimpact » Wed Jul 30, 2025 4:34 am Hmm. I don't think so, and I have a couple of ideas as to why. Though of course, feel free to disagree or pick up on things I may have missed: During 1.11, we see Shinji being given and drinking from a juice box after an Eva sortie, while speaking with Misato. If memory serves correctly, this actually happens a few times in early Eva. Given Ritsuko's insistence on the LCL being perfectly safe and Shinji being able to breathe it (thus indicating that as the chief science officer she has full confidence in it's qualities), I'm not sure if Shinji would be given a juice box after a sortie if the LCL was nourishing. Also, we have the issue of Shinji's stint in Leliel, during episode 16. Shinji reports being tired while trapped inside Leliel, which might also indicate that he wasn't able to eat any of the LCL for energy. Shinji was only in there for 16 hours; that's not so long to go without sleep, but it is a while to go without food, especially if you're 14. So, I reckon that Shinji was getting pretty hungry. I'm not sure if you can argue that Shinji's 28-day stint in the Eva during ep21 counts as proof it's nutritious either. Remember that Shinji didn't have a body in that episode, and therefore nothing to "power" in the same way food does. You could argue it nourishes "the soul", maybe? That's sort of nourishment. But again, doesn't really cover the body. My final reason, (and this is just theory crafting so REALLY ignore this unless you want to get funky); if it was nutritious or provided energy, that would indicate things like glucose (or at least some form of sugar) being present. You know what loooooooves glucose? Bacteria. Bacteria loves glucose. And could you imagine just how gross and moldy and rank the LCL deposits we see in the show would get if every bacterial culture in the world had the unabashed opportunity to chow down on a Giant Soup Made Of Sugar, for multiple weeks while people crawl out of it? By Lilith, it would get gross fast. Post-Instrumentality-Nerv would be fighting off the GIANT MOLD ISLAND THAT HAS FORMED IN THE PACIFIC. It's making me itchy just to think about... Yes. We all got out of Instrumentality better, but now the Greater Mold Colony has developed communication and is making Contact, and we might start to regret sending Unit One out in to space...
